summary: Blazing Scarlet Begonias and a killer Dancin' > Wharf Rat jam
blurb: The show shines with a scorching Scarlet Begonias and an electrifying Dancin' In The Street → Wharf Rat → Dancin' sequence in the second set. Jerry fires on all cylinders with crisp leads while Phil delivers a possessed bass performance. Overall it’s a solid 1976 outing that rewards attentive listening.
review: On October 14, 1976 at the Shrine Auditorium the Dead delivered a memorable night anchored by a blistering Scarlet Begonias that set the tone early in the first set. The band kept the momentum rolling through classics like Peggy‑O, Ramble On Rose, Looks Like Rain and The Music Never Stopped, but it was the second set that truly lifted the show: a seamless Dancin' In The Street → Wharf Rat → Dancin' In The Street medley that showcases the group's improvisational chemistry. Jerry Garcia’s guitar is described as “firing on all cylinders,” while Phil Lesh sounds almost possessed on bass, driving the jam forward with palpable intensity. Though the first set is captured only from audience mics and feels a touch less focused, the matrix recording (gd76-10-14.sbd.cotsman.12614.sbeok.shnf) provides clear SBD coverage of the second set and encore, making it the most complete listening experience despite minor EQ quirks and a short cut on Around & Around.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Both recordings are uniformly positive, highlighting a "blistering" Scarlet Begonias, an "electrifying" Dancin' > Wharf Rat > Dancin' medley, Jerry’s exceptional guitar work and Phil’s ‘possessed’ bass. Consistent praise across two sources warrants a solid positive boost.

best_recording.reason: Matrix capture includes full first set (AUD) and a high‑quality soundboard second set plus encore, delivering the most complete and clear representation of the performance despite minor EQ needs.

key_highlights: Scorching Scarlet Begonias that launches the show.
Electrifying Dancin' In The Street → Wharf Rat → Dancin' medley in Set 2.
Phil Lesh’s ‘possessed’ bass driving the jams.
Clear SBD capture of the second set makes the matrix recording the definitive version.

band_performance.Jerry: Fires on all cylinders with crisp, fire‑filled leads throughout the night.
band_performance.Phil: Sounds possessed on bass, delivering an inspired and driving low end especially during the Dancin' medley.
